sets.title: Complaint About a Delayed Furniture Delivery

passage.subject: Delayed Delivery — Order #44721
passage.body:
Dear Customer Service Team,

I am writing to express my disappointment with the handling of my recent order. On March 3rd, I purchased a dining table and six chairs from your Burnaby showroom (Order #44721). At the time of purchase, the salesperson, Mr. Davies, assured me that delivery would take place within two weeks. It has now been over a month, and I still have not received my furniture.

I called your customer service line twice last week. The first representative told me the items were 'in transit' but could not provide a tracking number. The second representative said the order had not yet left the warehouse and offered no explanation for the delay. Neither person followed up with me as promised.

This delay has caused real inconvenience. I scheduled a family gathering at my home next weekend specifically because I was told the table would arrive in time. I have now had to borrow folding tables from a neighbour, which is hardly the impression I hoped to make.

I would like a clear delivery date in writing within the next three business days. If you cannot guarantee delivery before next Saturday, I will expect a partial refund to compensate for the inconvenience, as well as confirmation that the delivery fee will be waived.

I have been a loyal customer of NorthPine for many years and have recommended your store to friends. I hope this matter can be resolved quickly so that I can continue to do so.

Sincerely,
Janet Morrison

mcq_questions.question_text: Why is Janet writing this email?

options.A: To complain about a delayed delivery and request action
options.C: To compliment Mr. Davies on his service
options.D: To ask about new products at the showroom

mcq_questions.question_text: When did Janet originally place her order?
What did the second customer service representative tell her?

options.A: The order was in transit with no tracking number
options.B: The order had not yet left the warehouse
options.D: The order would arrive next Saturday

mcq_questions.question_text: What can be inferred about Janet's family gathering?

options.A: It has been cancelled because of the delay
options.B: It will be hosted at a neighbour's home
options.C: It will go ahead but with borrowed tables instead
options.D: It depends on whether the furniture arrives

mcq_questions.question_text: What does Janet want from NorthPine within three business days?

options.B: A phone call from Mr. Davies

mcq_questions.question_text: How would you best describe the tone of Janet's email?

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read the response written to Janet. Complete the response by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Ms. Morrison,

Thank you for taking the time to contact us. I would like to begin by [BLANK_7] for the inconvenience you have experienced with Order #44721. The level of service you describe is not what we expect of our team, and I understand your frustration.

I have personally looked into your order this morning. I can confirm that your dining set is currently at our regional warehouse and is [BLANK_8] for delivery this Friday between 9:00 a.m. and noon. You will receive a confirmation call from our delivery team the day before.

In recognition of the delay, we will be [BLANK_9] the delivery fee in full and applying a 15% credit to your account, which can be used toward any future purchase. I have also spoken with Mr. Davies and the customer service representatives involved to ensure that follow-up procedures are improved going forward.

We truly value your [BLANK_10] as a long-time customer and your willingness to recommend us to others. Please do not hesitate to contact me directly at the number below if any further issues [BLANK_11] before Friday.

Kind regards,
David Chen
Customer Care Manager

passage.subject: Request for Extended Borrowing Period
passage.body:
Dear Library Staff,

My name is Daniel Park, and I recently moved to Oakville from Hamilton. I signed up for a library card last month and have been very impressed with the selection of books, especially the Korean-language novels.

However, I have found that the standard 14-day borrowing period is often too short for me. I work rotating shifts at a nearby hospital, and I sometimes cannot visit the library for two or three weeks at a time. I have already renewed several books online, and once I was charged a late fee because I forgot to renew before the deadline.

I would like to request an extended borrowing period of 21 days, if that is available. I take good care of every book I borrow and always return items in excellent condition. I would also be happy to sign up for email reminders if that is required.

Thank you for considering my request. I look forward to continuing to use the library.

Sincerely,
Daniel Park

mcq_questions.question_text: Why is Daniel writing this email?

options.A: To complain about a late fee he was charged
options.B: To apply for a new library card
options.D: To request a longer borrowing period

mcq_questions.question_text: Where did Daniel recently move from?
Why does Daniel find the 14-day period difficult?

options.B: His rotating work schedule makes regular visits hard
options.C: He borrows too many books at once
options.D: He lives far from the library

mcq_questions.question_text: What happened when Daniel forgot to renew a book?

options.C: He was charged a late fee
options.D: He had to buy a replacement

mcq_questions.question_text: What can be inferred about Daniel's attitude toward the library?

options.A: He values the library and wants to keep using it
options.B: He is frustrated and wants to cancel his membership
options.C: He thinks the library needs more books
options.D: He prefers buying books over borrowing

mcq_questions.question_text: How would you describe the tone of Daniel's email?

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read the response written to Daniel. Complete the response by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Mr. Park,

Thank you for reaching out and for your kind words about our [BLANK_7]. We are glad to hear you are enjoying the collection, including our Korean-language section.

I am pleased to let you know that we do offer an extended borrowing option of 21 days for patrons who [BLANK_8] for our Frequent Reader program. To enroll, simply bring your library card to the front desk and complete a short form. There is no additional [BLANK_9] for this service.

Once enrolled, all items you borrow will automatically have a 21-day [BLANK_10] period. We also encourage you to sign up for email and text reminders, which can be set up through your online account.

We appreciate your care with borrowed materials and hope to continue [BLANK_11] you for a long time.

Warm regards,
Helen Ng
Head Librarian, Oakville Public Library

passage.from: Sarah Chen, Block Captain — Elmwood Crescent
passage.subject: Annual Spring Cleanup & BBQ — Saturday, April 20
passage.body:
Dear Neighbours,

Spring is here, and it is time once again for our annual Elmwood Crescent Spring Cleanup! Last year, more than 40 residents came out and we collected over 60 bags of leaves and litter — a new record. Let us see if we can do even better this year.

The cleanup will take place on Saturday, April 20, from 9:00 a.m. to noon. Gloves, bags, and rakes will be provided, but feel free to bring your own gardening tools if you have them. We will focus on the boulevard, the park path along the creek, and the area around the community mailboxes.

Once the work is done, everyone is invited to a barbecue at my house (14 Elmwood Crescent) starting at 12:30 p.m. Burgers, hot dogs, and drinks will be provided. If you would like to bring a side dish or dessert, that would be wonderful but is certainly not required.

Please reply to this email by April 15 so I can plan for food. Children are welcome — we will have sidewalk chalk and bubbles to keep the little ones busy. I hope to see you all there!

Warm regards,
Sarah Chen

mcq_questions.question_text: What is the main purpose of Sarah's email?

options.A: To report a complaint about litter on the street
options.B: To sell tickets to a community fundraiser
options.C: To ask neighbours to donate gardening tools
options.D: To invite residents to a cleanup event and barbecue

mcq_questions.question_text: How many bags of litter were collected last year?
What time does the barbecue begin?
What does Sarah ask residents to do by April 15?
What can be inferred about the Elmwood Crescent community?

options.A: The cleanup is a well-established annual tradition
options.B: Most residents are unhappy with the neighbourhood
options.C: Sarah is being paid to organize the event
options.D: Only adults are expected to participate

mcq_questions.question_text: What is the overall tone of Sarah's email?

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read the reply from a resident. Complete the reply by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Hi Sarah,

Thanks so much for organizing this again! You really are the [BLANK_7] of the neighbourhood when it comes to bringing people together.

My husband and I will both be there at 9:00 a.m. sharp. I will bring a large garden rake and an extra pair of gloves in case anyone [BLANK_8] theirs. We found the creek path especially messy last year, so maybe we should [BLANK_9] a few extra people to that area this time.

For the barbecue, I would love to bring my potato salad — it is always a [BLANK_10] at family gatherings. Should I also bring paper plates, or do you have enough?

The kids are excited too. My daughter has been talking about the sidewalk chalk since you first [BLANK_11] the cleanup last month. See you on the 20th!

Best,
Jennifer

sets.title: Restaurant Apology for a Bad Experience

passage.from: Marco Rossi, General Manager — Bella Cucina Restaurant
passage.subject: Our Sincere Apologies for Your Recent Visit
passage.body:
Dear Ms. Howard,

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience at Bella Cucina last Saturday evening. I have read your comments carefully, and I want you to know that I take your concerns very seriously.

I understand that your pasta arrived cold, that you waited over 45 minutes for your main course, and that there was an error on your bill. None of this is acceptable, and I sincerely apologize. Saturday was an unusually busy night, but that is not an excuse for the level of service you received.

I have spoken with the kitchen team and the server who assisted you to make sure this does not happen again. I would like to invite you and a guest back for a complimentary dinner so that we can show you the experience Bella Cucina is known for. Please call me directly at 905-555-0173 to arrange a convenient date.

Thank you again for bringing this to my attention. Your feedback helps us improve.

With warm regards,
Marco Rossi

mcq_questions.question_text: Why is Marco writing this email?

options.A: To promote a new menu item
options.B: To respond to a job application
options.D: To apologize for a poor dining experience

mcq_questions.question_text: Which of the following was NOT a problem during Stephanie's visit?
How long did Stephanie wait for her main course?
What does Marco offer Stephanie?

options.D: A discount on her next visit

mcq_questions.question_text: Why does Marco mention that Saturday was unusually busy?

options.A: To explain, but not excuse, the poor service
options.B: To boast about the restaurant's popularity
options.C: To suggest Stephanie visit on a quieter night

mcq_questions.question_text: How would you describe Marco's tone?

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read Stephanie's reply to Marco. Complete the reply by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Mr. Rossi,

Thank you for your prompt and thoughtful [BLANK_7]. I appreciate that you took the time to acknowledge what happened and to follow up with your staff.

I was quite [BLANK_8] after our visit, especially because we had chosen Bella Cucina to celebrate my mother's birthday. However, your response has changed my impression of the restaurant, and I would be happy to [BLANK_9] your offer of a complimentary dinner.

I will call the number you provided to [BLANK_10] a date that works for us. I am hopeful that our next visit will be a much better [BLANK_11].

Thank you again,
Stephanie Howard

sets.title: Inquiry About a Summer Day Camp

passage.subject: Questions About the Junior Explorer Program
passage.body:
Dear Camp Sunshine Team,

I am writing to ask about the Junior Explorer Program for this coming summer. My daughter, Emma, is seven years old and has never attended a day camp before. She is very excited about the idea, but as a first-time camp parent, I have a number of questions.

First, could you confirm the age range for the Junior Explorer group? Your website says ages 6 to 9, but a friend told me it recently changed to ages 7 to 10. Second, what does a typical day look like? Emma is an active child who loves being outdoors, but she is also quite shy and may need some time to adjust to new groups.

I also need to know whether you can accommodate food allergies. Emma has a severe peanut allergy, and I need to be confident that the camp environment is safe for her. Do you have an allergy policy, and are the counsellors trained in using an EpiPen?

Finally, I was wondering about payment options. Is it possible to pay in two instalments rather than one lump sum? The full fee of $475 is manageable, but splitting it would be helpful for our family budget.

Thank you for your time. I look forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Lisa Thompson

mcq_questions.question_text: Why is Lisa writing this email?

options.A: To register her daughter for camp immediately
options.B: To complain about incorrect information on the website
options.C: To request a refund for last year's camp
options.D: To ask questions before enrolling her daughter

mcq_questions.question_text: Why is Lisa unsure about the age range?

options.B: The website and a friend gave different information
options.C: Emma is too old for the program
options.D: The camp changed its name recently

mcq_questions.question_text: What is Lisa's concern about Emma's personality?

options.A: Emma does not like outdoor activities
options.C: Emma may struggle to adjust because she is shy
options.D: Emma does not get along with other children

mcq_questions.question_text: What medical concern does Lisa raise?

options.A: Emma has a severe peanut allergy

mcq_questions.question_text: What can be inferred about Lisa's financial situation?

options.A: She can manage the cost but would prefer to split payments
options.B: She cannot afford the camp at all
options.C: She is looking for a free program
options.D: She wants to negotiate a lower price

mcq_questions.question_text: How would you describe Lisa's tone?

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read the response from Camp Sunshine. Complete the response by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Ms. Thompson,

Thank you for your interest in Camp Sunshine! I am happy to answer your questions and help you feel [BLANK_7] about sending Emma to us this summer.

The Junior Explorer group is for ages 6 to 9 — the website is correct. A typical day includes outdoor games, nature hikes, arts and crafts, and a supervised swim period. Our counsellors are experienced at helping shy children feel [BLANK_8], and we always pair newcomers with a buddy during the first week.

Regarding allergies, we take food safety very seriously. Our camp is completely nut-free, and all counsellors are [BLANK_9] in first aid, including EpiPen administration. We ask parents to complete a detailed medical form before the first day.

As for payment, we are happy to offer a two-instalment plan. The first [BLANK_10] of $237.50 is due at registration, and the second is due by June 15.

Please do not [BLANK_11] to reach out if you have more questions. We would love to have Emma join us!

Warm regards,
Amanda Gill
Camp Director

passage.from: Dr. Margaret Hayes, Department of Political Science — Dalhousie University
passage.subject: Letter of Recommendation — Priya Nair for the Leadership Scholarship
passage.body:
Dear Members of the Selection Committee,

I am writing to offer my strongest recommendation for Priya Nair, who is applying for the Pearson Leadership Scholarship. I have had the privilege of teaching and mentoring Priya over the past three years, and I can say without reservation that she is among the most intellectually curious and socially committed students I have encountered in my twenty-two years of university teaching.

Priya maintained a 3.92 GPA while simultaneously serving as president of the university's Model United Nations club and co-founding a peer tutoring program for first-year international students. What distinguishes her from other high achievers is the genuineness of her engagement: she does not pursue activities to build a résumé, but because she is deeply moved by questions of equity and access.

In my senior seminar on democratic governance, Priya produced a research paper examining voter suppression in municipal elections that was later accepted for presentation at the Canadian Political Science Association's annual conference — a rare achievement for an undergraduate. Her analysis was methodologically rigorous, carefully sourced, and written with a clarity that many graduate students struggle to achieve.

Beyond academics, Priya has volunteered with the Halifax Refugee Clinic for two years, assisting newcomers with housing applications and interpretation services. She has spoken publicly about the challenges facing refugee claimants, and her advocacy contributed to the clinic receiving additional provincial funding last year.

I have no doubt that Priya will use the Pearson Scholarship not merely to advance her own career, but to create meaningful change in whatever community she enters. She has my highest and most enthusiastic endorsement.

Sincerely,
Dr. Margaret Hayes

mcq_questions.question_text: What is the primary purpose of this email?

options.B: To recommend a student for a scholarship
options.D: To request funding for a research project

mcq_questions.question_text: What did Priya co-found at the university?

options.B: A peer tutoring program for international students

mcq_questions.question_text: What was notable about Priya's research paper?

options.A: It won a national essay contest
options.B: It was published in a book
options.C: It was accepted at a major academic conference
options.D: It was co-written with Dr. Hayes

mcq_questions.question_text: Why does Dr. Hayes mention that presenting at the conference is 'a rare achievement for an undergraduate'?

options.A: To emphasize how exceptional Priya's work is
options.B: To suggest Priya should switch to graduate studies
options.C: To criticize the conference's selection standards
options.D: To explain why she helped Priya with the paper

mcq_questions.question_text: What contribution did Priya make at the Halifax Refugee Clinic?

options.D: She helped newcomers with housing and interpretation

mcq_questions.question_text: What does Dr. Hayes imply about Priya's motivation for extracurricular activities?

options.A: Priya participates mainly to strengthen her applications
options.B: Priya is required to volunteer for her degree
options.D: Priya is genuinely driven by a concern for fairness

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read the committee's reply to Dr. Hayes. Complete the reply by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Dr. Hayes,

Thank you for your detailed and [BLANK_7] recommendation of Priya Nair. We receive many applications each year, and letters from faculty mentors are an essential part of our [BLANK_8] process.

Your description of Priya's academic achievements, particularly her conference presentation, clearly [BLANK_9] her from the typical applicant pool. We were also impressed to learn about her sustained commitment to the Halifax Refugee Clinic, which aligns closely with the Foundation's values of community [BLANK_10] and leadership.

We are now in the final stage of review and expect to notify all candidates by May 15. Should we require any additional information, we will not hesitate to [BLANK_11] you.

With appreciation,
Robert Fung
Chair, Pearson Academic Foundation Selection Committee

passage.subject: Recurring Heating Issues — Unit 4B, 210 Maple Road
passage.body:
Dear Property Management,

I am writing to report an ongoing heating problem in my apartment at 210 Maple Road, Unit 4B. For the past three months, the radiator in my living room has been making loud banging noises and frequently shuts off on its own, sometimes for hours at a time. On several nights in January, the indoor temperature dropped below 16°C, which I understand is below the minimum standard set by the city's property maintenance bylaw.

I reported this issue in early December and again in mid-January. A maintenance technician visited on both occasions and told me the problem was related to an aging boiler in the basement. Each time, the radiator worked for a few days after the visit and then stopped again.

This situation has been especially difficult because my daughter, who is four years old, has been getting frequent colds this winter. Our family doctor suggested that the cold indoor temperatures may be contributing to her illness.

I am requesting that the boiler be properly repaired or replaced as soon as possible. I would also appreciate a written timeline for when this work will be completed. If the issue is not resolved within the next two weeks, I may need to contact the Landlord and Tenant Board for assistance.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
James O'Brien

mcq_questions.question_text: Why is James writing this email?

options.A: To request a rent reduction for the winter months
options.B: To report recurring heating problems and demand a fix
options.C: To give notice that he is moving out
options.D: To ask about a new apartment in the building

mcq_questions.question_text: How long has the heating problem been going on?
What did the maintenance technician say was causing the problem?

options.C: An aging boiler in the basement
options.D: A faulty thermostat in the unit

mcq_questions.question_text: What happened after each maintenance visit?

options.A: The radiator worked briefly and then stopped again

mcq_questions.question_text: Why does James mention his daughter's health?

options.A: To ask the landlord to pay her medical bills
options.B: To explain why he cannot pay rent this month
options.C: To request a transfer to a warmer unit
options.D: To show that the heating problem is causing real harm to his family

mcq_questions.question_text: What is James implying by mentioning the Landlord and Tenant Board?

options.A: He plans to move out immediately
options.B: He will take formal action if the problem is not fixed soon
options.C: He has already filed a complaint
options.D: He wants to become a member of the board

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read the response from the property manager. Complete the response by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Mr. O'Brien,

Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention again, and please accept my [BLANK_7] for the ongoing inconvenience. I understand how frustrating it must be, especially with a young child at home.

After reviewing the maintenance reports from December and January, I agree that the temporary repairs have not been [BLANK_8]. I have authorized the replacement of the basement boiler, and our contractor has confirmed that the work can begin this Thursday. The installation is expected to take two to three days, during which we will provide [BLANK_9] space heaters for your unit at no charge.

I have also arranged for a building inspector to [BLANK_10] the heating system once the new boiler is in place to ensure everything meets city standards. You will receive a written confirmation of the completion date by email.

Please do not hesitate to call me directly if you have any further [BLANK_11]. I want to make sure your family is comfortable.

Sincerely,
Diane Foster
Sunrise Property Management

sets.title: Farewell Lunch Invitation for a Coworker

passage.subject: Farewell Lunch for Kevin — Friday, March 14
passage.body:
Hi everyone,

As most of you know, Kevin Chandra's last day at Horizon Media will be next Friday, March 14. After five wonderful years on our team, Kevin is moving to Calgary to be closer to his family. We will miss him greatly!

To send him off properly, I am organizing a farewell lunch at Trattoria on King Street. The reservation is for 12:30 p.m., and I have booked the back room so we can have some privacy. The restaurant offers a set lunch menu for $22 per person, which includes an appetizer, main course, and coffee or tea.

I am also collecting contributions for a goodbye gift. If you would like to chip in, please send me $10 by e-transfer or bring cash to my desk by Wednesday. I am planning to get him a gift card to his favourite outdoor gear store.

Please let me know by Tuesday if you can make it so I can confirm the headcount with the restaurant.

Thanks!
Rachel

mcq_questions.question_text: Why is Rachel writing this email?

options.C: To invite the team to a birthday party
options.D: To ask for help with a project

mcq_questions.question_text: Why is Kevin leaving the company?

options.A: He was offered a promotion elsewhere
options.B: He is retiring after a long career
options.C: He is moving to Calgary to be near family
options.D: He is unhappy with his current role

mcq_questions.question_text: How much does the set lunch cost per person?
What gift does Rachel plan to buy?

options.A: A gift card to an outdoor gear store

mcq_questions.question_text: Why did Rachel book the back room?

options.A: It is cheaper than the main dining area
options.C: The restaurant requires it for large groups
options.D: So the group can celebrate privately

mcq_questions.question_text: What is the tone of Rachel's email?

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read a colleague's reply to Rachel. Complete the reply by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Hi Rachel,

Thanks for putting this together — Kevin definitely [BLANK_7] a proper send-off. Count me in for Friday! I love Trattoria, so that is a great choice.

I will send you $10 by e-transfer tonight for the gift. An outdoor gear card is a perfect idea since Kevin is always talking about his [BLANK_8] trips. Maybe we can also sign a card? I feel like a group message would be a nice personal [BLANK_9].

One quick question — is parking available near the restaurant, or should I plan to take the [BLANK_10]? I do not want to be late trying to find a spot.

It is hard to believe Kevin has been here for five years already. He has been such a great [BLANK_11] to work with. Calgary is lucky to have him!

See you Friday,
Tom

sets.title: Employee Request for Modified Work Schedule

passage.to: Human Resources Department, Clearview Financial Group
passage.subject: Request for Modified Work Hours — Effective April 1
passage.body:
Dear HR Team,

I am writing to formally request a modification to my daily work schedule, beginning April 1, if possible. I have been with Clearview Financial for four years and currently work the standard hours of 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., Monday through Friday.

My wife recently accepted a nursing position with shifts that start at 7:00 a.m. This means that I will now be the one responsible for dropping our three-year-old daughter off at daycare each morning. The daycare opens at 7:30 a.m., but it takes me approximately 40 minutes to drive from there to the office during rush hour. As a result, I would not be able to arrive before 9:15 most mornings.

I would like to propose starting my workday at 8:30 a.m. and finishing at 4:30 p.m. This half-hour shift would allow me to drop off my daughter on time and still complete a full eight-hour day. I have discussed this arrangement with my supervisor, David Liu, who has indicated that he is supportive as long as HR approves it.

I want to emphasize that this change would not affect my productivity or availability for team meetings, which typically begin at 10:00 a.m. I am committed to maintaining the same level of performance that I have always brought to my role.

Thank you for considering this request. I am happy to discuss it further at your convenience.

Best regards,
Ahmed Hassan

mcq_questions.question_text: What is the main reason Ahmed is writing this email?

options.B: To ask for a change in his daily work hours
options.C: To report a problem with his supervisor

mcq_questions.question_text: Why has Ahmed's morning routine changed?

options.A: He moved to a new neighbourhood
options.C: His wife took a job with early morning shifts
options.D: The office relocated to a different building

mcq_questions.question_text: What schedule does Ahmed propose?
What has Ahmed's supervisor said about the request?

options.B: He wants Ahmed to work from home instead
options.C: He has not yet been told about it
options.D: He supports it if HR approves

mcq_questions.question_text: Why does Ahmed mention that team meetings start at 10:00 a.m.?

options.A: To suggest the meetings should be rescheduled
options.B: To complain that meetings are too early
options.D: To show that his proposed schedule would not cause conflicts

mcq_questions.question_text: Which word best describes Ahmed's approach in this email?

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read HR's response to Ahmed. Complete the response by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Ahmed,

Thank you for your request regarding a modified work schedule. We appreciate you [BLANK_7] this matter with your supervisor before reaching out to us.

After reviewing your proposal, I am pleased to confirm that your request has been [BLANK_8]. Starting April 1, your work hours will officially change to 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. This arrangement will be reviewed after a three-month [BLANK_9] period to ensure it continues to work well for both you and the team.

Please note that all employees on modified schedules are expected to remain [BLANK_10] by phone and email during core business hours, which are 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. If you need to be away during those hours for any reason, standard leave procedures apply.

We value your dedication over the past four years and are happy to [BLANK_11] your family needs wherever possible. Please feel free to contact me with any questions.

Best regards,
Natalie Park
HR Coordinator

sets.title: Formal Complaint About a Home Renovation

passage.subject: Formal Complaint — Kitchen Renovation, Contract #GBR-2024-1187
passage.body:
Dear Customer Relations Department,

I am writing to formally document my dissatisfaction with the kitchen renovation project currently being carried out at my home (Contract #GBR-2024-1187). What was presented as a straightforward four-week job has now stretched into its ninth week, with significant deficiencies in both workmanship and communication.

The tile work behind the stove is visibly uneven, with grout lines that vary in width by as much as five millimetres. When I raised this with the site foreman, Mr. Garrett, he dismissed my concern by saying it would 'look fine once the backsplash dries.' I subsequently had an independent contractor assess the work, and he confirmed that the tiles would need to be removed and re-laid.

More troubling is the countertop issue. The contract clearly specifies Calacatta quartz, yet the material installed is a lower-grade marble that chips easily and requires regular sealing — a significant downgrade that was never discussed with me. When I contacted your office about this, I was told the substitution was made because the specified material was 'backordered,' but no one sought my approval or offered alternatives.

Additionally, the crew has consistently left debris, open paint cans, and tools scattered across my backyard, raising safety concerns. My neighbour's child stepped on a loose nail near my fence line last week, fortunately without serious injury.

I am requesting a detailed remediation plan within seven business days, including a timeline for correcting the tile work, replacing the countertop with the specified material, and cleaning the work site daily going forward. Should these issues not be addressed satisfactorily, I intend to file a complaint with the Ontario Ministry of Public and Business Service Delivery and seek cost recovery through Small Claims Court.

Sincerely,
Catherine Dubois

mcq_questions.question_text: What is the primary purpose of Catherine's email?

options.A: To request an extension to the renovation timeline

passage.subject: Important Update: Festival Postponed to November 9
passage.body:
Dear Festival Guests,

It is with great regret that I must inform you of the postponement of the Autumn Harvest Festival, originally scheduled for this Saturday, October 26. Due to a severe weather warning issued by Environment Canada — with heavy rain, strong winds, and potential flooding expected throughout the weekend — we have made the difficult decision to move the event to Saturday, November 9.

The safety of our guests, vendors, and volunteer staff is our top priority, and we simply cannot risk holding an outdoor event under these conditions. The main stage, food court, and children's activity zone are all open-air areas with limited shelter, and setting up in high winds would put our team at risk.

I know this is disappointing. Many of you have been looking forward to the pumpkin carving contest, the live bluegrass performances, and the local cider tasting — and so have we. I want to assure you that every activity on the original program will be offered on November 9, and we are adding a bonus attraction: a farm-to-table cooking demonstration by Chef Alicia Fontaine.

All tickets remain valid for the new date. If you are unable to attend on November 9, you may request a full refund through our website by October 31. We have also extended the early bird discount for an additional week for anyone who would like to bring a friend.

Thank you for your understanding, and I sincerely apologize for the inconvenience.

Warm regards,
Derek Singh

options.C: To inform attendees that the festival has been moved to a new date
options.D: To request more volunteers for the event

Happily, November 9 works for our family, so we will keep our tickets. My kids were a bit disappointed at first, but when I told them about the cooking [BLANK_9] with Chef Fontaine, they got excited all over again.

I also appreciate you extending the early bird discount. I have already forwarded your email to a couple of friends who were on the [BLANK_10] about buying tickets. Hopefully they will join us now.

Thank you for handling this so [BLANK_11]. We know it is not easy to reschedule something this big.

Best,
Karen

passage.subject: Information About English Language Programs
passage.body:
Dear Pinecrest Centre,

My name is Maria Santos, and I arrived in Canada from Brazil four months ago. I live in the Scarborough area of Toronto with my husband and our two young children.

I studied some English in school, but I find it difficult to have conversations in everyday situations such as visiting the doctor, speaking to my children's teachers, or shopping at the grocery store. I would like to take English classes to improve my speaking and listening skills.

Could you please let me know what programs you offer for newcomers at my level? I am especially interested in classes that focus on practical, everyday English rather than academic writing. I am available on weekday mornings while my children are at school.

I also want to ask whether childcare is available during classes. My younger son is two years old and is not yet in daycare. If childcare is offered, I may be able to attend afternoon sessions as well.

Thank you very much for your help. I am looking forward to hearing from you.

Sincerely,
Maria Santos

mcq_questions.question_text: Why is Maria writing this email?

options.A: To apply for a job at the settlement centre
options.B: To register her children for school
options.C: To ask about English classes for newcomers
options.D: To complain about a service she received

mcq_questions.question_text: How long has Maria been in Canada?
What kind of English does Maria want to focus on?
Why is Maria asking about childcare?

options.A: She wants a job as a childcare worker
options.B: Her older child does not have a school
options.D: She needs someone to watch her two-year-old during class

mcq_questions.question_text: What can be inferred about Maria's current English level?

options.B: She has some basics but struggles with real-life conversations
options.C: She has never studied English before
options.D: She speaks English at a professional level

mcq_questions.question_text: How would you describe Maria's tone?

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read the reply from the settlement centre. Complete the reply by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Maria,

Thank you for contacting us, and welcome to Canada! We are happy to help you find the right English [BLANK_7] for your needs.

Based on your description, our LINC (Language Instruction for Newcomers to Canada) program at the CLB 3–4 level would be a great [BLANK_8]. These classes focus on everyday communication skills — such as making appointments, talking to teachers, and understanding public notices — exactly what you described.

Morning classes run Monday to Thursday from 9:30 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. We do offer free on-site childcare for children under four, so your younger son would be [BLANK_9] while you are in class.

To get started, you will need to complete a language [BLANK_10] at our centre so we can place you in the right level. Assessments are available every Tuesday morning. Please bring your permanent resident card or other immigration documents.

We look forward to [BLANK_11] you soon!

Warm regards,
Jasmine Ali
Program Coordinator, Pinecrest Settlement Services

sets.title: Professional Reference for a Social Worker

passage.from: Dr. Susan Webb, Clinical Director — Harbourview Mental Health Services
passage.subject: Professional Reference — Karen Liu, MSW, RSW
passage.body:
Dear Hiring Committee,

I am writing in response to your request for a professional reference for Karen Liu, who has applied for the Social Worker position at Riverside Health Centre. Karen worked under my supervision at Harbourview Mental Health Services for four years, from 2020 to 2024, and I can provide an unqualified endorsement of her clinical abilities and professional character.

During her time at Harbourview, Karen managed a caseload of approximately 35 to 40 clients, many of whom presented with complex, co-occurring conditions including severe anxiety disorders, substance use, and trauma histories. She consistently demonstrated the ability to build therapeutic rapport quickly, even with clients who had previously disengaged from services. Her documentation was thorough, timely, and reflective of sound clinical reasoning.

One area where Karen particularly excelled was interdisciplinary collaboration. She worked closely with psychiatrists, occupational therapists, and community outreach workers to develop integrated care plans, and she frequently took the lead in coordinating case conferences. Her colleagues often noted that she elevated the quality of discussion by asking precise, client-centred questions that kept the team focused on outcomes rather than process.

Karen also showed exceptional composure in crisis situations. On more than one occasion, she de-escalated volatile encounters in the walk-in intake area with a combination of calm authority and genuine empathy. Following one particularly difficult incident involving a client in acute psychosis, Karen developed a revised safety protocol for the intake team that was adopted agency-wide.

I consider Karen to be among the top five social workers I have supervised in my career. She would be a significant asset to your team, and I am confident she will bring the same dedication and professionalism to Riverside that she demonstrated here.

Please do not hesitate to contact me if you require any further information.

Sincerely,
Dr. Susan Webb, PhD, RSW

options.C: To provide a professional reference for a job applicant
options.D: To announce a new safety protocol

options.D: She only worked with mild cases

options.A: An endorsement written by someone without qualifications

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read the hiring committee's reply to Dr. Webb. Complete the reply by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Dr. Webb,

Thank you for your exceptionally thorough reference for Karen Liu. Your insights into her clinical skills and professional [BLANK_7] have been very helpful to our committee.

We were particularly impressed by your description of her crisis [BLANK_8] skills and her initiative in developing a new safety protocol. These are qualities we value highly at Riverside, where our team handles a significant volume of intake and emergency cases.

Based on the strength of her application and your recommendation, we would like to [BLANK_9] Karen for an interview panel next week. We will contact her directly with scheduling details.

Should we require any [BLANK_10] information during the evaluation process, we will reach out to you again. We greatly appreciate the time and care you put into this reference — it is clear that Karen made a lasting [BLANK_11] during her years at Harbourview.

With thanks,
Dr. Nadia Osman
Chair, Hiring Committee — Riverside Health Centre

sets.title: Request for a Traffic Safety Measure

passage.to: City of Burlington — Traffic Safety Division
passage.subject: Request for Speed Bumps on Willow Street
passage.body:
Dear Traffic Safety Division,

I am writing on behalf of the residents of Willow Street to request the installation of speed bumps or an additional stop sign on our street. We have become increasingly concerned about vehicle speeds in our neighbourhood, particularly during school drop-off and pick-up hours.

Willow Street runs directly past Lakeview Elementary School and has no traffic calming measures between Pine Avenue and the school entrance — a distance of roughly 400 metres. Despite the posted 40 km/h speed limit, many vehicles travel well above this during the morning rush. Last month, a child on a bicycle was nearly struck by a speeding van while crossing at the school crosswalk. Fortunately, the child's parent pulled him back just in time.

I have circulated a petition among our neighbours and collected 34 signatures from households on Willow Street and the surrounding blocks, all supporting the installation of speed bumps. The petition is attached to this email.

We are not asking for a major infrastructure project — just two or three well-placed speed bumps between Pine Avenue and the school would make a meaningful difference. We would also welcome a community meeting with your team to discuss other options, such as a flashing speed sign or additional crosswalk markings.

Thank you for considering our request. Our children's safety is our primary concern.

Sincerely,
Paul Andrews
Willow Street

mcq_questions.question_text: Why is Paul writing this email?

options.A: To request traffic calming measures near a school
options.C: To object to a new road construction project
options.D: To apply for a crossing guard position

mcq_questions.question_text: What is the speed limit on Willow Street?
What near-accident occurred recently?

options.A: A school bus ran a red light
options.B: Two cars collided at the intersection
options.C: A pedestrian fell on the sidewalk
options.D: A child on a bicycle was nearly hit by a speeding van

mcq_questions.question_text: How many signatures did Paul collect?
Why does Paul say 'We are not asking for a major infrastructure project'?

options.A: To suggest that he does not care about the issue deeply
options.B: To discourage the city from doing more than speed bumps
options.C: To show the request is modest and reasonable
options.D: To imply that the city wastes money on large projects

mcq_questions.question_text: What is the effect of Paul mentioning the near-accident involving a child?

options.A: It shows he is exaggerating the problem
options.B: It proves that the speed limit should be lowered to 20 km/h
options.C: It gives a concrete example that makes the safety concern urgent
options.D: It suggests that the school should move to a safer location

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read the city's response to Paul. Complete the response by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Mr. Andrews,

Thank you for writing to us about traffic safety on Willow Street. We take concerns related to school zones very [BLANK_7], and I appreciate you taking the time to organize a petition from your neighbours.

After reviewing your request, I can confirm that Willow Street has been added to our list of priority locations for a traffic [BLANK_8]. A team from our division will visit the area next week to collect speed data, observe traffic patterns during school hours, and assess the roadway for suitable speed bump [BLANK_9].

Once the study is complete — typically within three to four weeks — we will share the results with you and [BLANK_10] our recommendations. If speed bumps are warranted, we can usually begin installation within two months of approval.

I would also be happy to arrange the community meeting you [BLANK_11]. I will follow up with some possible dates early next week.

Best regards,
Susan Lam
Traffic Safety Coordinator, City of Burlington

sets.title: Condo Owner Protests a Special Assessment

passage.to: Board of Directors, Lakeshore Terrace Condominium Corporation
passage.subject: Objection to Special Assessment — Resolution 2024-07
passage.body:
Dear Board of Directors,

I am writing to formally object to the special assessment of $15,000 per unit approved under Resolution 2024-07 at last month's board meeting. While I understand that the lobby renovation is intended to improve the building's appearance and property values, I have serious concerns about the process by which this decision was made, the scope of the proposed work, and the financial burden it places on owners.

First, the notice of the meeting at which this resolution was passed was sent just ten days in advance, giving owners insufficient time to review the renovation proposal, obtain independent cost estimates, or organize a meaningful discussion. Under the Condominium Act, matters involving a special assessment of this magnitude should, in my view, require a broader consultation process, including a town hall meeting open to all owners.

Second, the proposed budget of $1.2 million for a lobby renovation appears excessive. I have spoken with two licensed contractors who estimate that comparable work — new flooring, lighting, and furniture — could be completed for approximately $650,000 to $800,000. The board has not provided a breakdown of costs, nor has it indicated whether competitive bids were solicited. Transparency on this point is essential.

Third, many owners in this building are retirees on fixed incomes. A $15,000 lump-sum payment with only 60 days' notice creates genuine financial hardship. I would urge the board to consider alternatives such as a phased payment plan over 12 months, a scaled-back renovation focusing on safety-critical items first, or the use of reserve fund contributions to offset part of the cost.

I request that the board table Resolution 2024-07, schedule a town hall meeting within 30 days, and provide a full cost breakdown and evidence of competitive bidding before proceeding. I reserve the right to bring this matter before the Condominium Authority Tribunal if my concerns are not addressed.

Sincerely,
Natasha Volkov

mcq_questions.question_text: What is Natasha's main purpose in writing this email?

options.A: To object to a special assessment and request changes to the process
options.B: To volunteer for the renovation committee
options.C: To propose a completely different renovation plan
options.D: To resign from the condo board

mcq_questions.question_text: How much is each unit being assessed?
What does Natasha say about the notice period for the meeting?

options.B: She received no notice at all
options.D: It gave owners ten days, which she feels was not enough

mcq_questions.question_text: What do independent contractors estimate the renovation could cost?
Why does Natasha mention that many owners are retirees on fixed incomes?

options.A: To suggest they should be exempt from the assessment
options.B: To recommend that only retirees vote on the resolution
options.C: To argue that the payment timeline creates genuine financial hardship
options.D: To propose that the building should be sold

mcq_questions.question_text: What does Natasha's mention of the Condominium Authority Tribunal imply?

options.A: She is prepared to pursue legal remedies if the board does not respond
options.B: She has already lost a case before the tribunal
options.C: She believes the tribunal will automatically side with her

fill_in_blank_response.instructions: Now read the board chair's response. Complete the response by filling in the blanks. Choose the best option for each blank.
fill_in_blank_response.response_text:
Dear Ms. Volkov,

Thank you for your detailed letter regarding Resolution 2024-07. The board takes your [BLANK_7] seriously, and I appreciate you raising several points that warrant further discussion.

You are correct that the notice period was shorter than ideal. While the ten-day notice met the minimum legal requirement under the Condominium Act, I [BLANK_8] that a matter of this financial significance deserved more lead time. We will ensure broader consultation going forward.

Regarding costs, the board did receive three bids from licensed contractors. The winning bid included structural upgrades to the lobby pillars that were not part of the purely cosmetic estimates you may have [BLANK_9]. We will circulate a full cost breakdown to all owners within the next two weeks.

I am also open to your suggestion of a phased payment plan. We will explore the [BLANK_10] of offering a 12-month instalment option and will include this topic on the agenda for a town hall meeting, which I am scheduling for November 15.

I hope this response demonstrates our willingness to address your concerns in good [BLANK_11]. We look forward to your participation in the upcoming meeting.

Best regards,
Graham Ellis
Board Chair, Lakeshore Terrace Condominium Corporation
